confidence_rationale: 2024 review from the Buchel lab integrating control-over-pain effects into a Bayesian pain model. Places anterior insula, MFG, and ACC as the substrate of control-modulation, linked via precision-weighted expectations. Supports the MECH-258 framing (precision-weighted PE). Lower weight because it is review-level.
failure_signatures: A raw-magnitude model of pain-into-action-value (not precision-weighted) is argued against by the control literature this review synthesises.
Does not directly address stream-substrate sharing.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Control over pain modulates perceived pain via changes in expectation and their precision. The anterior insula, middle frontal gyrus, and ACC are the neurobiological substrate. The effect is predicted by a Bayesian pain model with precision-weighted integration.
mapping.ree_translation: Supports the precision-weighted PE formulation in MECH-258: 'PE = z_harm_a_observed - E2_harm_a(...); dACC_input = precision * PE'. The review brings control (our SD-032c-ish signal) into the same Bayesian model, which is architecturally consistent with a shared-substrate forward model whose precision is modulated by ACC/AIC signals.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Does not directly test within-pain stream separation. Relevant to MECH-258 formulation more than to the shared-vs-parallel decision.

confidence_components.notes: High-quality venue, but review-level. Mapping is primarily to MECH-258's precision-weighting, not the sharing decision.
